action: Monitor system performance and analyze process execution times
details: Use monitoring tools to track system resource utilization, CPU usage, memory consumption, and network bandwidth. Analyze the execution times of processes with varying timeout values to identify patterns or correlations with system resource constraints or network issues.

action: Check error logs and exception reports for clues on root cause
details: Run the command: cat /var/log/error.log | grep 'timeout' to check for any error messages related to timeouts. Also, run the command: grep 'exception' /var/log/exception.log to identify any exception reports that may indicate the root cause of the issue.

action: Investigate database or storage issues as a potential root cause
details: Run the command: ps aux | grep 'database' to check if any database-related processes are consuming excessive resources. Also, run the command: du -h /var/lib/database to check for any storage issues that may be impacting database performance.
